title: Iran targets M365 accounts with password-spraying attacks
Android Developer Verification Rollout Begins Ahead of September Enforcement
summary: Google is rolling out Android developer verification globally to combat harmful apps and anonymous bad actors, with a mandate beginning in select countries this September.

title: US indicts Maryland man for 2021 theft of $54 million from Uranium Finance
Cisco source code stolen in Trivy-linked dev environment breach
summary: Cisco experienced a cyberattack where credentials from a recent Trivy supply chain breach were used to compromise their internal development environment and steal source code belonging to Cisco and its customers.

title: Attack on axios software developer tool threatens widespread compromises
summary: Researchers are issuing warnings about a supply-chain attack on axios, a widely-used open-source software developer tool with 100 million weekly downloads, which poses a threat of widespread compromises.

title: TrueConf Zero-Day Exploited in Attacks on Southeast Asian Government Networks
summary: A high-severity zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-3502) in TrueConf video conferencing software is being exploited in the wild, dubbed TrueChaos, to target government networks in Southeast Asia by distributing tampered application updates.

title: New criminal service plans to monetize data stolen by ransomware gangs
How to Categorize AI Agents and Prioritize Risk
summary: This article explains how CISOs can categorize AI agents based on their system access and autonomy to effectively prioritize cybersecurity risks and secure them.

title: Hackers compromise Axios npm package to drop cross-platform malware
summary: Hackers compromised the npm account of the widely used Axios JavaScript package to distribute remote access trojans, impacting Linux, Windows, and macOS systems.

title: Hackers Poison Axios npm Package with 100 Million Weekly Downloads
Vertex AI Vulnerability Exposes Google Cloud Data and Private Artifacts
summary: A newly discovered vulnerability in Google Cloud's Vertex AI platform could allow attackers to weaponize AI agents, gaining unauthorized access to sensitive data and compromising an organization's cloud environment.

title: CISA tells federal agencies to patch Citrix NetScaler bug by Thursday
Microsoft fixes Outlook Classic crashes caused by Teams Meeting add-in
summary: Microsoft has resolved a known issue where the Teams Meeting Add-in caused the classic Outlook email client to crash and become unusable for users.

title: The AI Arms Race – Why Unified Exposure Management Is Becoming a Boardroom Priority
summary: The article highlights the rapidly accelerating cybersecurity landscape, driven by the weaponization of AI, which is leading to dramatically faster attacks and exploitation, making unified exposure management a critical boardroom priority.

title: Silver Fox Expands Asia Cyber Campaign with AtlasCross RAT and Fake Domains
summary: The Silver Fox group is expanding its cyber campaign in Asia, targeting Chinese-speaking users with typosquatted domains that distribute a new remote access trojan called AtlasCross RAT, impersonating various trusted software applications.

title: Critical F5 BIG-IP Flaw Upgraded to 9.8 RCE, Exploited in the Wild
Supply chain blast: Top npm package backdoored to drop dirty RAT on dev machines
Hacker charged with stealing $53 million from Uranium crypto exchange
summary: U.S. prosecutors have charged a Maryland man with stealing over $53 million from the Uranium Finance crypto exchange through two hacks and laundering the stolen funds via a cryptocurrency mixer.

title: Dutch Finance Ministry takes treasury banking portal offline after breach
summary: The Dutch Ministry of Finance has taken its digital treasury banking portal and other systems offline to investigate a cyberattack detected two weeks ago.

title: CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Citrix flaw by Thursday
summary: CISA has ordered U.S. federal agencies to patch an actively exploited Citrix NetScaler vulnerability by Thursday due to its immediate threat.

title: Axios Supply Chain Attack Pushes Cross-Platform RAT via Compromised npm Account
summary: The popular Axios npm package suffered a supply chain attack via compromised versions (1.14.1 and 0.30.4) that injected a malicious dependency, delivering a cross-platform trojan capable of targeting Windows, macOS, and Linux systems.

title: AI Agents Are Democratizing Finance but Also Redefining Risk
Kernel Observability for Data Movement
Pro-Russian hackers pose as Ukraine's cyber agency to target government, businesses
Healthcare tech firm CareCloud says hackers stole patient data
summary: Healthcare IT firm CareCloud has disclosed a data breach incident where hackers stole patient data and caused an eight-hour network disruption.

title: OpenAI Codex Vulnerability Allowed Attackers to Steal GitHub Tokens
New RoadK1ll WebSocket implant used to pivot on breached networks
OpenAI patches ChatGPT flaw that smuggled data over DNS
Critical Citrix NetScaler memory flaw actively exploited in attacks
summary: Hackers are actively exploiting a critical memory flaw, CVE-2026-3055, in Citrix NetScaler ADC and Gateway appliances to obtain sensitive data.

title: Researchers say credential-stealing campaign used AI to build evasion ‘at every stage’
summary: A credential-stealing campaign, named DeepLoad, is reportedly using AI to generate evasion techniques at every stage, including obfuscating code, logging keystrokes, and re-infecting hosts after initial blocks.

title: OpenAI Patches ChatGPT Data Exfiltration Flaw and Codex GitHub Token Vulnerability
Wave Browser Brings Gaming Tools and Ocean Cleanup into the Same Tab
Italian regulator fines financial giant $36 million for data protection failures
Telnyx joins LiteLLM in latest PyPI package poisoning tied to Trivy breach
15-Year-Old strongSwan Flaw Lets Attackers Crash VPNs via Integer Underflow
24/7 Payments for 24/7 Agents: The Case for Crypto in the Machine Economy

⚡ Weekly Recap: Telecom Sleeper Cells, LLM Jailbreaks, Apple Forces U.K. Age Checks and More
summary: This weekly cybersecurity recap details long-running operations finally surfacing, old attack methods re-emerging in new contexts, and research becoming practical, covering topics from telecom sleeper cells to LLM jailbreaks and policy changes.

title: Citrix NetScaler bug exploited in days, may be multiple flaws in a trench coat
Dark Web Market Lists Alleged 375TB Lockheed Martin Data for $600M
3 SOC Process Fixes That Unlock Tier 1 Productivity
summary: The article highlights that inefficiencies in SOC Tier 1, such as fragmented workflows and limited visibility, are major causes of delays and unnecessary escalations, rather than just the threats themselves. It proposes process fixes to improve productivity and the overall SOC response.

title: TeamPCP Uses Fake Ringtone File in Tainted Telnyx SDK to Steal Credentials
European Commission admits attackers broke into public web systems, but says little else
Security contractor blew the whistle on support crew's viral indifference
US foreign router ban criticized for being ‘industrial policy disguised as cybersecurity’
Iranian hackers, Handala, claim to compromise FBI Director Kash Patel’s personal data
summary: Iranian hacker group Handala claims to have compromised the personal data of FBI Director Kash Patel via his personal email, though the FBI confirmed awareness and stated no government information was taken.

title: Security leaders say the next two years are going to be ‘insane’
summary: Leading security experts warn that the next two years will be 'insane' due to AI accelerating bug discovery and exploit development, with most organizations being unprepared for the escalating cyber threat landscape.

title: AFC Ajax drops ball as flaws let hackers play admin with tickets and bans
summary: AFC Ajax experienced security flaws that granted hackers administrative privileges, allowing them to manipulate ticketing systems and user bans within their platforms.

title: Iran war drives urgent need to counter underwater attack drones
ODNI tackles AI, threat hunting, app cybersecurity in year-one tech review
FCC pushes new rules to crack down on robocallers, foreign call centers
Former NSA chiefs worry American offensive edge in cybersecurity is slipping
summary: Former NSA chiefs are concerned that the U.S. is losing its offensive cybersecurity edge, citing a systemic numbness to cyberattacks and ever-widening threats, with warnings that the worst cyber day is still ahead.

title: Alleged RedLine infostealer conspirator extradited to US
summary: An Armenian man has been extradited to the US to face charges for allegedly administering the RedLine infostealer, described as one of the most prevalent infostealing malware variants globally.

title: Google moves post-quantum encryption timeline up to 2029
DarkSword’s GitHub leak threatens to turn elite iPhone hacking into a tool for the masses
